ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Number of hours since last drank anything except water
Number of hours since last food intake
How would you describe the amount of caffeine you had in the last 24 hours?
Notes: 1 = The usual amount; 2 = Less than the usual amount; 3 = More than the usual amount; 4 = Not applicable

ElementDescription: Number of hours since last had caffeine/stimulants
Have you received any vaccine in the last two weeks?

Notes: 1 = Unable to obtain blood (Greater than 2 sticks); 2 = Patient request to stop (pain, paresthesia); 3 = Patient condition (lightheaded, fainted); 4 = Hematoma after needle stick (bruising, ecchymosis); 5 = Other

ElementDescription: Time completed blood draw: days since surgery
Notes: Difference between time of blood draw and surgery date

ElementDescription: Time completed centrifuging: days since surgery
Notes: Difference between time of centrifuging and surgery date

ElementDescription: Assess the degree of hemolysis of the plasma (Lavender tube) after centrifugation according to the laminated hemolysis color scale, and record the estimated hemoglobin value in g/L.
Time plasma aliquot tubes placed in freezer
Time plasma aliquot tubes placed in freezer: days since surgery
Notes: Difference between freezer time and surgery date
1 = Unable to obtain blood sample - technical reason; 2 = Unable to obtain blood sample - patient related; 3 = Sample handling/processing error
